what_it_does: This file is an adversarial verification ledger for proposed type-system rules: it opens cited slide sources, checks whether each rule is visible in the pixels, and records a verdict of STANDS, CORRECTED, or REFUTED along with source evidence and a corrected recipe when needed.
observations: Contains 10 verdict entries covering hero-figure sizing, figure-to-label contrast, content budgets, an 8-role type table, one-line invariants, emphasis registers, conversational titles, single-type family, extra-bold weight anchor, and type.scale confirmation.
All four corrections are flagged as confirming-bias in 'extends-existing' claims.
Hero-figure 60.75px is corrected to an observed ~48-56px on the landing canvas.
Figure-to-label contrast is corrected from the imposed 1.5^k {2,3}=2.25-3.375x to the observed ~2:1 to ~4:1 range.
Role-table figure colour is corrected from bright accent.gold #E3C421 to muted ochre-gold matching the source pixels.
The method note scopes absolute px and 700-vs-800 weight claims because rasters are downscaled composites.

Several rules are marked STANDS on pixel evidence: one-line discipline with carve-outs, bold-vs-gold emphasis registers, claim titles, no-serif single family, heavier hero weight, and type.scale within tolerance.
standouts: The figure-colour correction is the strongest signal: opened slides show section heads and figures in the same muted ochre, refuting the bright-gold 'two-tone refinement' claim.
The hero-figure size correction rejects an unmeasured geometric next-note (60.75px) in favor of observed source values.
The contrast-range correction replaces an elegant 1.5^k formula with the wider, source-recorded ratio spread.
conventions: Follows a regular verdict object schema: name, status_claimed, verdict, confirming_bias, source_evidence, corrected_recipe.
Uses neutral, evidence-first language and explicitly tags when a correction stems from confirmation bias.
Records measurement caveats honestly rather than overstating raster precision.
Distinguishes corpus-direct phenomena from inferred formulas.
concerns: Some verdicts marked STANDS still rest partly on inferred formulas (e.g., zone-width char budget computation), though flagged as inferred.
The method admits absolute px and exact weight are not measurable, yet the file still reports approximate ratios and colour hex comparisons.
A cited calibration title is noted to be 60 chars rather than the claimed 62, but the rule is allowed to stand.
Source evidence entries are long and argumentative, which could make automated parsing harder.
notes: A pixel-grounded audit of proposed type rules that mostly confirms the rules but pushes back on four exact recipes that overfit geometric or color inferences.
questions: What is the exact upstream proposal file these verdicts are verifying?
How are the confirming_bias flags and verdict counts consumed by downstream fusion or governance?
What schema governs corrected_recipe when it is empty versus non-empty?
Where are the definitions of 'new' versus 'extends-existing' rule categories maintained?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is an adversarial source-check ledger for type rules, producing verdicts and corrected recipes grounded in opened slide evidence.
apparent_themes: Reject overfit geometric formulas while keeping real phenomena
Preserve source-muted palette over live bright tokens
Separate corpus-direct rules from inferred computations
intention_signals: The file is trying to stop design-token drift by forcing every proposed type rule back to the opened slide pixels, downgrading unsupported exact numbers and color claims while keeping the underlying visual principles intact.
novelty: The default-refuted adversarial stance, explicit confirming-bias tagging, and corrections that preserve the observed phenomenon but reject the proposer's precise recipe are unusual for a design-token review file.
contribution: A source-grounded verdict set with corrected recipes for hero-figure size, figure-to-label contrast range, and figure colour, plus explicit caveats about raster measurability that a fused system would keep to avoid reintroducing unsupported exact values.
summary_for_embedding: A verified ledger of adversarial source-check verdicts for proposed type-system rules, recording 6 stands, 4 corrected recipes, and 0 refutations against opened slide rasters.
intention_for_embedding: To ground proposed typography rules in visible slide pixels and correct overfit size, ratio, and color recipes before they enter the fused design substrate.
